data.title: Harmony Bio slips on late-stage trial setback for Fragile X syndrome therapy
data.description:
• Harmony Biosciences (NASDAQ:HRMY) lost ~6% in the premarket on Wednesday after the company announced the failure of a registrational Phase 3 clinical trial for ZYN002, its experimental therapy for a rare neurobehavioral condition known as Fragile X syndrome.
  • Based on topline data, the Pennsylvania-based biopharma said that due to a higher-than-expected placebo response rate, its RECONNECT Study didn’t reach the primary endpoint related to social avoidance, which is an intellectual disability seen in patients with FXS.
  • The multicenter trial evaluated ZYN002, a cannabidiol-based therapy designed as a transdermal gel, in patients aged 3–30 years with FXS. Despite the setback, Harmony (NASDAQ:HRMY) said that it will analyze the full dataset from RECONNECT, given the high unmet medical need for FXS treatments.

data.title: Birchtech secures license agreement with large regional power utility company
data.description:
• Birchtech (TSX:BCHT:CA) on Wednesday announced a non-exclusive agreement with a large regional coal-fired power utility that the company filed suit against in January 2025 regarding the company’s patented mercury emissions reduction technologies.
  • The suit against this utility had been consolidated with the other cases that were originally filed in 2024 for pre-trial matters in Lowa.
  • The license agreement with the regional utility includes a one-time license fee. Other specific terms of the agreement cannot be publicly disclosed due to confidentiality agreements.
  • Birchtech has dismissed all claims against this particular utility in the consolidated lawsuits currently pending in Lowa.
  • Claims will continue against the remaining defendants in the consolidated Lowa actions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Aeries Technology announces major enterprise client expansion
data.description:
• Aeries Technology (NASDAQ:AERT)on Wednesday announced the expansion of an engagement with a recently signed global enterprise client.
  • The expanded scope is expected to drive the client relationship to an annualized revenue run-rate exceeding $12 million by Aeries’ fiscal year-end, i.e. end of March 2026, with additional growth anticipated in subsequent quarters.
  • The client is broadening its utilization of Aeries’ delivery platform across multiple functions, including artificial intelligence, technology services, finance, and customer support, leveraging Aeries’ delivery hubs in India and Mexico.
  • AERT +9.33% premarket to $0.9699.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: DeFi Development announces $100 million share repurchase authorization
data.description:
• DeFi Development (NASDAQ:DFDV) announced on Wednesday that its Board of Directors has approved an increase to the company’s existing stock repurchase program from $1 million to up to $100 million of its common stock.
  • The company noted that the repurchase authorization provides flexibility to repurchase up to $100 million of common stock, with an initial $10 million threshold requiring management to update the Board prior to executing additional purchases.
  • DFDV +3.83% premarket to $15.69.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Uniti Group announces refinancing transactions
data.description:
• Uniti Group (NASDAQ:UNIT) on Wednesday said its subsidiary, Windstream Services has commenced an offering of $900M aggregate principal amount of senior secured notes due 2033.
  • The issuer is also pursuing up to $1,500,000,000 aggregate principal amount of incremental term loan borrowings under the legacy Windstream credit agreement and an extension of the maturity dates of its revolving credit facilities under the legacy Uniti credit agreement and legacy Windstream credit agreement to December 30, 2027, effective upon receipt of customary regulatory approvals.
  • The issuer intends to use the net proceeds from the offering and the borrowings under the 2025 Term Loan to fund the redemption in full of its outstanding 10.50% senior secured notes due 2028 and to pay any related premiums, fees and expenses, including accrued and unpaid interest, if any.
  • Any remaining proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes.
  • UNIT +0.89% premarket to $6.73.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: NFL's Goodell wants to renegotiate TV rights with media partners in early 2026
data.description:
NFL Commissioner Roger Goodell said Wednesday the league could begin renegotiating terms for its television rights with media partners as soon as 2026, four years earlier than the current agreement’s opt-out clause.

In an exclusive interview with CNBC, Goodell said the NFL is leaving money on the table after observing other recent sports media deals.

To revisit terms, the league would need to convince its current media partners Disney (NYSE:DIS), NBCUniversal (NASDAQ:CMCSA), Paramount (NASDAQ:PSKY), Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), and Fox (NASDAQ:FOX) (NASDAQ:FOXA) to start discussions on any new deal.

Back in 2021, the NFL entered into an $111B, 11-year rights deal that includes an opt-out clause after the 2029-30 season with all of its media partners except Disney, which has one extra year of rights. 

"I think our partners would want to sit down and talk to us at any time, and we continue to dialogue with them. I like that opportunity," Goodell told CNBC. "Obviously it’s not going to happen this year. But it could happen as early as next year. That could happen."

"The reason why we felt so strongly about the option is the landscape is changing. It could be a long-term deal with the benefit of having that stability and security of it. But I think the reality of it is it changes so quickly that you want to have the ability to move. I think those options are going to give us a lot of flexibility to potentially go earlier," Goodell told CNBC.

If the NFL manages to secure a new deal, it could also hike the salary cap in future seasons, giving teams more money to spend on players and expand the roster.

data.title: EGG Media confirms Sports.com to acquire Racing Women assets in $1M deal
data.description:
• EGG Media (NASDAQ:SEGG) on Wednesday confirmed that Sports.com Media Group has signed a letter of intent to invest in women's sports by acquiring all the assets of Racing Women.
  • Under the LOI, Sports.com Media will become the rights holder to Racing Women and acquire a 51% controlling stake in a newly created entity that holds all the assets of Racing Women at a $1 million valuation, with options to expand to full ownership.
  • The acquisition includes Racing Women’s intellectual property and global rights, establishing the initiative as a core asset within SEGG Media’s motorsport expansion strategy.
  • EGG Media (NASDAQ:SEGG) shares rose nearly 5% in premarket trading.

data.title: WNS launches new AI platform for transforming finance organizations
data.description:
• WNS Limited (NYSE:WNS) announced  on Wednesday the launch of Agile Target Operating Model (aTOM), a proprietary AI-led digital platform for driving end-to-end transformation of global finance organizations. 
  • The platform delivers transformation across the entire F&A value chain, including Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, General Ledger, FP&A, and Tax & Treasury.
  • Early collaborations with clients using aTOM have resulted in cost savings of approximately 40%, productivity enhancements of more than 50%, improved working capital, and increased AI adoption, the company said. 
  • WNS also plans to leverage the aTOM platform into other organizational functional areas beyond finance.

data.title: Nuvini Group launches NuviniAI index
data.description:
• Nuvini Group (NASDAQ:NVNI) announced on Wednesday the launch of the NuviniAI Index, a showcase, and performance tracker of AI-driven tools designed to assess the level of AI adoption in organizations through an objective questionnaire. 
  • The initiative aims to drive internal portfolio transformation and enhance external M&A qualification.
  • For current portfolio companies, the NuviniAI Index serves as a management and transformation framework. 
  • Each company is scored and benchmarked against industry peers and the broader NuviniAI ecosystem, creating an objective baseline for investment decisions. 
  • Nuvini Group (NASDAQ:NVNI) shares were up more than 3% in pre-market trading.

data.title: Rezolve AI secures $200M oversubscribed financing
data.description:
• Rezolve AI (NASDAQ:RZLV) announced it has signed agreements with two existing institutional investors for an oversubscribed $200 million private placement (before fees and expenses).
  • The deal is expected to close around September 25, 2025, pending customary conditions.
  • The company plans to use the funds to invest for accelerated investment into its sales organization, pursue potential M&A, and support working capital and general corporate needs.
  • RZLV +1.67% premarket to $7.3
  • Source: Press Release
